title: High-Risk Country CNP Surge with Residential Proxy Masking
narrative: Card issued in US suddenly shows 8-12 CNP transactions over 48 hours, all routing through IP addresses geolocated to Nigeria, Indonesia, or Romaniaâ€”countries flagged for organized CNP fraud rings. TransactionAmt values cluster tightly at $87-$94, suggesting automated scripting to stay below merchant fraud thresholds. The dist1 feature shows 8,000+ km distance between billing address (addr1) and transaction IP geolocation. All purchases occur during txn_hour 2-6 AM cardholder local time, typical of overseas fraudsters operating in their own business hours. P_emaildomain shows free webmail services like yahoo.com or outlook.com despite card history of corporate email domains. ProductCD remains 'W' (web-based services/digital goods) across all transactions, avoiding physical shipping verification. Card1_txn_count_24h spikes from baseline of 0.3 to 11, while card1_amt_zscore exceeds 4.2 standard deviations. No CVV mismatch errors occur, indicating full card data compromise rather than card-testing behavior.
indicators: 8-12 transactions within 48 hours from IP addresses in Nigeria, Indonesia, or Romania
dist1 exceeds 8,000 km between billing addr1 and transaction geolocation
All txn_hour values between 2-6 AM in cardholder's timezone
P_emaildomain switches to free webmail (yahoo.com, outlook.com) from prior corporate domains
card1_amt_zscore exceeds 4.2 standard deviations above historical mean
summary: A U.S.-issued card suddenly processes 8-12 identical-value transactions ($87-$94) within 48 hours, all routed through high-risk country IPs (Nigeria, Indonesia, Romania) over 8,000 km from the billing address during the cardholder's nighttime hours (2-6 AM), with the email switching from corporate to free webmail.
reasoning: Organized fraud rings use stolen card data and residential proxies in high-risk jurisdictions to rapidly extract value through scripted purchases that stay just under merchant review thresholds, timing attacks when the legitimate cardholder is asleep and unlikely to notice alerts.
indicator_explanations: Burst of transactions originating from known high-risk fraud jurisdictions
All purchases cluster around same dollar amount suggesting automated testing
Transaction location is thousands of miles from card's billing address
Activity concentrated during cardholder's typical sleeping hours
Email domain suddenly changes from business to anonymous free service
Card goes from nearly dormant to extremely active overnight
Purchase amounts far exceed what this cardholder normally spends
